assertions.title: Brief activity items open the matching job drawer

steps.action: Capture dashboard state with overdue Daily Brief activity visible
steps.expected: An activity-feed item is present for an overdue follow-up
steps.observed: Daily Brief showed "One follow-up has gone unanswered past its window" and an activity item for MariaDB.

steps.expected: The matching MariaDB drawer opens from the right rail
steps.observed: The MariaDB drawer opened with matching title/company and the same role visible in the drawer header.

steps.expected: The dashboard returns to the same readable state
steps.observed: Escape closed the drawer and returned the board/brief to the same dashboard view with the activity item still visible.

assertions.title: Signed-in users can edit CRM fields from the drawer

steps.action: Load marker-state auth bootstrap and inject runtime OAuth session fields
steps.expected: Authenticated write-capable drawer controls remain available for real writes
steps.observed: The UI temporarily hid Continue, showed signed-in account chrome, and exposed CRM controls in the MariaDB drawer.

steps.action: Attempt a real Google API auth probe with the bridge token
steps.expected: userinfo and Sheets API return 200 for the same validator token
steps.observed: Both https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v3/userinfo and a Sheets spreadsheet probe returned 401.

steps.action: Attempt an in-drawer CRM save during the authenticated-looking state
steps.expected: CRM edit persists without session loss
steps.observed: The prior save attempt collapsed back to signed-out mode with a session-expired toast, so the UI-auth bootstrap was not backed by valid write auth.

assertions.issues: Runtime token bridge exposes write controls briefly, but the underlying Google token is invalid for real API writes, so signed-in CRM editing cannot be validated in Exec mode.

assertions.title: Status changes move jobs between the correct lanes

steps.action: Prepare authenticated drawer state for MariaDB
steps.expected: Status can be changed and persisted to the sheet
steps.observed: CRM controls appeared only during a temporary injected-auth state.

steps.action: Verify the token bridge can actually write to Google APIs
steps.expected: Google APIs accept the validator token
steps.observed: userinfo and sheet probes both returned 401, matching the earlier session-expired save failure.

assertions.issues: Blocked by invalid Google auth in the validator session; cannot persist a status change to verify lane movement.

assertions.title: CRM edits update visible dashboard signals

steps.action: Seed overdue signal and open MariaDB drawer
steps.expected: An authenticated follow-up edit can be saved and reflected back into the brief
steps.observed: The overdue signal is present, but save attempts depend on the same invalid token bridge.

steps.action: Probe Google auth backing the injected drawer controls
steps.expected: Token is valid for Sheets writes
steps.observed: Both auth probes returned 401, and the earlier in-drawer save kicked the UI back to Continue/sign-in state.

assertions.issues: Blocked by invalid Google auth; visible overdue signals exist, but a real follow-up edit cannot be persisted to prove live signal updates.

assertions.title: Truly empty pipelines show guided empty states

steps.action: Inspect the documented empty-pipeline fixture metadata
steps.expected: A real header-only sheet id is available for the empty-pipeline run
steps.observed: The empty fixture documentation still uses EMPTY_PIPELINE_SHEET_ID_PLACEHOLDER instead of a real sheet id.

steps.action: Try the alternate candidate sheet that was available during validation
steps.expected: The app loads a truly empty pipeline
steps.observed: The alternate sheet rendered a populated board with 15 roles, not an empty pipeline.

assertions.issues: Blocked by missing real empty-pipeline fixture; the documented placeholder was never replaced and the alternate sheet was populated.

assertions.title: Open drawers stay in sync after in-drawer saves

steps.action: Expose CRM controls via injected auth and keep the drawer open
steps.expected: A real in-drawer save keeps the drawer attached to the same role
steps.observed: CRM controls appeared, but the backing session was not actually authorized.

steps.action: Probe the validator token against Google APIs
steps.observed: userinfo and Sheets probes both returned 401, consistent with the earlier session-expired save attempt.

assertions.issues: Blocked by invalid Google auth; cannot complete a real in-drawer save to verify drawer/board sync.

frictions.description: Injected auth can momentarily expose write-mode drawer UI even when the backing Google token is invalid.
frictions.resolution: Confirmed invalidity with direct userinfo/Sheets 401 probes and treated write assertions as blocked instead of over-claiming success.

frictions.description: The empty-pipeline fixture remains incomplete because the documented sheet id is still a placeholder.
frictions.resolution: Tested the alternate candidate sheet and confirmed it was populated, leaving VAL-DASH-013 blocked.

blockers.description: Token-bridge auth is not valid for real Google API writes in the validator session.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Injected accessToken/grantedOauthScopes into runtime, confirmed UI-auth chrome, then verified the token with direct Google probes; both userinfo and Sheets returned 401.

blockers.description: No real empty-pipeline sheet fixture is wired for VAL-DASH-013.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Checked the documented empty fixture metadata and tested the alternate sheet id; both paths failed to provide a header-only pipeline.

summary: Round 4 fully validated VAL-DASH-002 and gathered stronger blocker evidence for the remaining auth/write assertions. The activity item → drawer flow passes end-to-end, but write assertions remain blocked because the token bridge is UI-only and Google API probes return 401. Empty-pipeline validation is still blocked by the unresolved fixture placeholder.
